observations.managementAction: The CDRRMO shall be requested to present the status of all approved and funded PPAs to ensure that the needed services are delivered efficiently and ensure that the comprehensive Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan (LDRRMP) serves as a basis in the allocation of funds. The CAO will draw journal entries on unrecorded PPE items in the General Fund books and ensure proper transfers from TF to GF upon issuance to end-users.
observations.reason: The following observations were noted in the current year audit: unrecorded PPE accounts still included unrecorded PPE accounts. The observations were included in the current year audit, viz: included unrecorded PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: a. Observe and regularly review the procedures for amending the Schedule of Fair Market Value of real properties and adhere to the appraisal/assessment calendar prescribed by the Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations; b. Establish a systematic and transparent process for conducting assessments, including the use of updated valuation methodologies and technologies; c. Strengthen information and dissemination campaign activities to raise citizens’ awareness of the need to revise the City Tax Ordinance and urge their support and involvement; and d. Once the proposed Ordinance has been enacted, publicize the approved SFMV in a newspaper of general circulation in the locality or posting in the city hall and two other conspicuous public places.
observations.managementAction: The Schedule of Market Values (SMV) was submitted to the SP for approval and adoption. A preliminary public hearing was conducted and the first reading was done on Oct. 5, 2023. The City Assessor’s Office is in the assessment process and will adopt standardized Schedule of Market Values (SMVs) in line with RPVARA (R.A. 12001) and coordinate with DOF-BLGF. Once approved, the LGU commits to publicize the same in a general circulation newspaper and conspicuous public places.
observations.reason: The proposed schedule of FMV had been withdrawn. With the implementation of RPVARA R.A. 12001, the LGU will adopt a standardized Schedule of Market Values to be approved by DOF and BLGF.

observations.recommendation:
a) Through the CGSO, submit the required reports for Road Networks,
including the Report on Local Road Network (RLRN), and maintain a
comprehensive Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC) for all roads
and their respective components, as prescribed in COA Circular No.
2015-008;
b) Through the CAO, to improve asset management and reporting by:
i. Regularly updating the Local Road Network Ledger Cards (LRNLC)
and ensure these are prepared for each road and their components,
rather than being consolidated by barangay;
ii. For the CGSO to coordinate with the City Engineering Office (CEO) to
expedite the completion of the Local Road Map and ensure the full
implementation of the geotagging initiative for all road networks,
integrating this data into existing infrastructure records;
c) To ensure proper recording of infrastructure-related expenditures
moving forward, the CGSO, CAO, and CEO must coordinate to establish
a mechanism for accurately classifying and monitoring infrastructure-
related expenditures;
d) For all concerned offices to explore the use of a database tool for road
networks to enhance data integration, facilitate real-time monitoring, and
support data-driven decision-making in infrastructure management; and
e) For the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to establish a dedicated Road
Inventory Committee that will provide the CGSO with technical expertise
and assistance in the proper inventory, valuation, and effective
management of the CGK’s road network assets, in compliance with the
Local Road Asset Management Manual and COA Circular No. 2015-008.

With the foregoing observations, we recommended the following to the
Management:
a) Prioritize compliance with the directives of COA Circular No. 2020-006 on
the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to ensure adherence to
prescribed accounting and reporting standards;
b) Reassess and strengthen the CGK’s internal controls, policies, and
procedures for the recognition, monitoring, safeguarding, and reporting
of PPEs to ensure asset records are complete and accurate;
c) Ensure that all donated properties are properly documented with a deed
of donation, duly signed by the donor and donee, and accompanied by
all necessary supporting documentation. Additionally, ensure that
copies of these documents are submitted to the relevant offices for
proper record-keeping;
d) Establish the prescribed account titles and implement appropriate
recording procedures for semi-expendable properties in compliance with
COA Circular Nos. 2022-004 and 2024-006. Ensure proper classification
upon acquisition and issuance, and verify that these transactions are
duly supported by the required documentation to facilitate accurate
financial reporting and reinforce asset accountability; and
e) Ensure that the PPE account balances are reconciled on a regular basis
by the City Accountant and City General Services Officer, and that any
discrepancies are promptly investigated and resolved. Appropriate
adjustments should be made as needed to ensure that accounts are
valued accurately in the financial statements.

a) Examine the components of the Due from GOCCs-PhilHealth account as
of December 31, 2024 to determine the collectability of the previously
recognized receivables;
b) Through the KCH and CHO PhilHealth Sections:
• Prepare the Summary of PhilHealth Claims (SSPH) which provides details
on the amount of collections that pertain to the previous years (Per year
report) and adjust the Due from GOCCs-PhilHealth Account accordingly;
• Create a detailed monthly monitoring report with patient information,
claim amount, filing date and the PhilHealth payments;
• Submit monthly the SSPH and relevant documents to the CAO for
accurate recording of PhilHealth claims and collections;
• Conduct periodic reconciliation of accounts with the PhilHealth Regional
Office to carry out the requirements of items 3 to 7, Section V-A of
PhilHealth Circular No. 2023-0028 and confirm the correct balance as of
December 31, 2024; and
c) Through the CAO, to timely and properly record the accrual of claims,
denied claims, and return to hospital claims under the above-mentioned
Circular, and to closely coordinate with the KCH and CHO PhilHealth
Section to address concerns as efficiently as practicable.

We recommended that Management:
a) Require concerned departments to revisit the appropriate guidelines
embodied by such funds transferred. May develop project proposals for
which received awards can be utilized;
b) Implement the corresponding PPAs funded by grants and internal
sources immediately, and timely submit the liquidation reports (LRs) to the
Source Agencies (SAs);
c) Refund excess project fund to the respective SAs or otherwise request for
transfer to its General Fund (GF), whichever is applicable, in accordance
with the agreement and terms of grants; and
d) Consistently monitor the implementation of PPAs to ensure proper
documentation and accounting thereof, maximizing the benefits to be
obtained by the constituents.

We recommended that Management:
a) Assess whether the originally funded PPAs are feasible to be
implemented, otherwise may consider reprogramming to more suitable
PPAs in consonance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and other
pertinent guidelines to encompass the four (4) thematic areas of DRRM,
such as disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness,
disaster response, and disaster rehabilitation and recovery. Integrate
them into the AIP for approval of the local Sanggunian to serve as a
roadmap in DRRMF utilization;
b) Develop PPAs that are more responsive to the community’s needs in
these rising times of disasters and calamities, health concerns and the
effects of uncontrolled increase of generated solid wastes;
c) Reconcile the utilization reports for DRRMF prepared by CAO and
CDRRMO periodically; and
d) Through the CAO, draw journal entries on the unrecorded PPE items in
the General Fund books and be recognized with depreciation charges.
Moving forward, ensure that PPE accounts are properly recorded in the
TF books and transfer them in the GF once completed, issued to the end-
user and ownership has been transferred in adherence to COA Circular
2012-002.

We recommended that Management:
a) Revisit the CGK’s planning and budgeting processes. Adopt the
procedures, systems and tools required by the DILG-NEDA-DBM-DOF
JMC No. 1 S. of 2016, item 7.0 for planning and budgeting and the different
applicable guidelines set forth therein;
b) Integrate planning and budgeting strategies such as, but not limited to,
Performance Informed Budgeting (PIB) that promotes public financial
management in the formulation of PPAs and budgets to ensure its
harmonization and that these truly operationalize the local development
plans; and
c) The CGK, through the LFC, may begin by conducting a detailed analysis
of expenditures over the previous two or three years, breaking down the
program activities it intends to initiate, and developing realistic expense
projections, thereby reducing the issuance of augmentation and
realignment orders and increasing budget reliability.

We recommended that Management:
a) Remind the component barangays of the deadlines for submitting the
barangay development plan, budget and program documents as well as the
potential administrative and criminal liability imposed on Punong Barangays
that fail to submit the budget as required by Section 318 of the R.A. No. 7160
and other relevant statutes;
b) May organize a team to spearhead the development and conduct of a
capacity-building workshop for the barangay in the preparation of BDPs,
AIPs, and budget accountability forms for the upcoming year to facilitate
ease of review; and
c) Ensure diligent monitoring of barangay compliance with the standards for
effective project, program and activity (PPA) implementation that is
responsive to the needs of its constituents while remaining aligned with the
over-all CGK vision, and make continuous progress in governance
practices and development initiatives.

a. For the General Services Office to strengthen their physical controls and improve procedures in the management of the properties and equipment. Actual physical count is being conducted every December of every succeeding period; • Conduct of complete and proper physical count of properties at least annually; • Maintenance of up-to-date records, according to applicable guidelines and regulations, to monitor the existence, condition, and movement of PPEs at any given time; • Render the required reports for Road Networks, maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC) for all roads and their components, with the support of the City Engineering Office; and • Periodic review of insurance coverage of all insurable assets to ensure that the City’s properties are adequately protected against any damage to or loss due to fire, earthquake, storm, or other fortuitous events/casualty in accordance with COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018.
b. For the City Accounting Office to enhance practices in the reporting of accounts by: • Making the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the appropriate accumulated depreciation amount and come up with the correct value of the PPE accounts; • Revisiting all IIRUP together with the transmitted disposal documents, and creating the necessary journal entries to drop from the books all PPE items that have already disposed of and demolished; and • Disclosing the total Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements to provide the end users with adequate information regarding the accounts following the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008.
c. For the City Accountant and the General Services Officer to ensure that the balances of the PPE accounts in their respective records are reconciled on a regular basis and that discrepancies noted shall be immediately investigated and resolved to maintain the proper valuation of accounts. Furthermore, both Offices shall collaborate their efforts and practice regular coordination to warrant a more accurate report of PPE balances.
observations.managementAction:
b. For the City The CAO already made adjusting entries to recognize appropriate accumulated depreciation amount and disposed PPEs. Further, relevant details of Road Networks account have already been disclosed in the Notes to FS.
c. For the City Accountant and the General Services Officer to ensure that the balances of the PPE accounts in their respective records are reconciled on a regular basis and that discrepancies noted shall be immediately investigated and resolved to maintain the proper valuation of accounts.
The CGSO also agreed to provide the CAO with all the necessary documents whenever adjustments were required owing to donations, disposals, or other transactions affecting PPE balances. Stringent monitoring of properties will also be implemented to ensure the proper reconciliation of balances across PPE records.
The CGK’s eNGAS system has already been updated recently to establish account titles related to semi-expendable properties. The CAO will now proceed with the reclassification of accounts to properly reflect the new capitalization threshold of the PPE.
observations.reason:
The following observations were noted in the current year audit, viz:
• Non-submission of required reports for Road Networks
• Non-maintenance of up-to-date PPE records

observations.recommendation:
a. For the City Accounting Office to communicate and request documents such as amortization schedules with banks and other lending institutions, to facilitate the amortization of the loans availed by the LGU and the proper identification of the current and non-current portion of the loans and other liability accounts, otherwise, use estimates based on past principal and interest payments and other applicable sources of information as basis in the computation, reclassification and recognition of loans payable and accrued interest; and
b. Consider assigning a focal team to oversee loan documentation compliance from start to approval, project implementation, and monitoring. Duplication efforts will be minimized as the team focuses on connecting with lending institutions to ensure current compliance and adequate record-keeping.
observations.managementAction:
The CAO already made adjusting entries to recognize appropriate accumulated depreciation amount and disposed PPEs. Further, relevant details of Road Networks account have already been disclosed in the Notes to FS.
The City Treasurer was designated as focal person to oversee loan documentation compliance, project implementation status and conduct monitoring. Duplication efforts will be minimized as the team focuses on monitoring.
observations.reason: The City Treasurer was designated as focal person to oversee loan documentation compliance and monitoring.

observations.managementAction: The DepEd Schools Division Board shall regularly assess the PPAs funded under the SEF. The DepEd Schools Division Office will be informed of the expenditures which should be funded by its DepEd general sources as listed in the AOM and 2024-06 (23) dated April 17, 2024. The government will continue to collaborate with DepEd on the budgetary and utilization procedures.
observations.reason: Not implemented: Budget for CY 2024 needs further assessment to address more pressing concerns of the learners in the locality.

observations.recommendation:
a. Through the CHO, KCH, and super health centers: • Develop a control mechanism to ensure that inventories and properties are documented on time and that the complete recording and proper monitoring of medical supplies and equipment are achieved; • Designate a permanent bonded supply officer to oversee and monitor the hospital's supplies, property and equipment, as well as the urban and super health centers; • Prepare and submit monthly reports embodying information as to the receipt, issuances and ending balances of medicines, medical supplies and other inventory items stored in its premises in a timely manner; and • Facilitate the design and/or construction of suitable storage facilities for medical supplies to ensure quality, safety, and systematic release/distribution to patients and program recipients.
b. Through the City Accounting Office, make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the supplies and equipment received from DOH and other partner agencies for proper accounting treatment and record-keeping;
c. Evaluate its existing financial situation and plan. The LGU may use tools such as project prioritization matrix to analyze and rank potential projects or initiatives based on specified criteria and objectives; and
d. Through the Local Finance Committee (LFC): • May come up with income-generating project proposals to supplement the existing needs of its constituents, particularly the delivery of health services through the KCH, CHO and the additional management of health centers in the barangays; and • Intensively seek additional funding from national government agencies (NGAs), Office/s of the House of Representatives, PhilHealth and other nongovernmental or private sectors to ensure sustainability and effective delivery of health services; and
e. Implement measures for public involvement through the Sangguniang Panlungsod, as it is a fundamental component in promoting the city's sustainable health and healthcare systems.
observations.managementAction:
The CHO has developed a control to ensure the complete recording and proper monitoring of receipts, issuance and inventories and ending balance through the installation of ELMIS. The office has requested for the plantilla position of Supply Officer who is tasked to oversee and monitor the supplies, and inventory count of medical supplies and other items is conducted quarterly, monthly and furnished to the CAO and GSO.
Journal entries on the receipt of supplies and equipment from DOH were already drawn.
Storage areas for medicines and supplies were already being used. The CHO is conducting monthly review of financial performance to intensively implement those projects that are generating more income and spending less expenses. Measures are being taken to replenish the income resources used in the delivery of service, including implementation of E-Konsulta package and seeking assistance from DOH, and in CY 2025, three (3) Super Health Centers were granted the License to Operate (LTO) by the DOH which is a key requirement for the application of packages.
observations.reason: Though inventory count of medical supplies and other items are conducted quarterly, monthly report on the ending balance is not prepared and submitted to the CAO and GSO.

observations.recommendation: a. Consider prioritizing the adoption of the septage management ordinance as it could initiate plans and actions for the implementing of the septage management system in the locality; b. Intensify collaborative efforts with the MKWD in pursuit to provide a clean and sufficient water supply in the area; c. May team up with relevant agencies, such as Department of Environment and Natural Resources’ Environmental Management Bureau (DENR-EMB), Department of Health (DOH) for hospital waste management, and other LGUs for knowledge sharing and benchmarking; and d. Organize seminars or educational initiatives aimed at implementing water conservation measures, advocating for the adoption of water-saving technologies, and offering incentives to encourage both households and businesses to reduce their water usage and proactively.
observations.managementAction: The CENRO’s proposed septage management discussions with City Administration indicated possibility that implementation of the Environment Code will start in the 3rd quarter of 2025. The City intensified collaborative efforts with MKWD and conducted information, education and communication activities. Seminars and IEC activities were implemented and scheduled in January 2025. The City has implemented discussions related to water conservation in their IEC activities and planned further implementations.
observations.reason: Not implemented: the schedule for implementing the Environment Code and full septage management adoption is still being finalized; further actions are ongoing.

observations.recommendation: b. For the City Engineering Office to assist and coordinate with the CAO as to the proper valuation of replaced projects. and revisit the policy in establishing the estimated useful life for purposes of derecognition in the books of accounts; and revisit the policy in establishing the estimated useful life for an infrastructure asset considering significant factors.
observations.managementAction: The CAO established a useful life of 10 or 20 years for road concreting projects. as efforts were concentrated in the implementation of road concreting projects as targeted for CY 2023.
observations.reason: Per CAO, the CEO has not yet coordinated with them regarding the proper valuation of replaced infrastructure assets; The CEO has yet to provide the CAO with further details regarding the expected useful life of road concreting projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to the
Management:

a. For the General Services Office to strengthen their controls and improve
procedures in the management of the City Government of Kidapawan’s properties
and equipment. Among the physical controls that may be adopted by the Office
are the following:

i. Conduct of complete and proper physical count of properties at least annually;
ii. Maintenance of required reports and up-to-date records, according to
applicable guidelines and regulations, to monitor the existence, condition, and
movement of PPEs at any given time;
iii. Render the required reports for Road Networks, maintain a Local Road
Inventory and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property
Card for all roads and its components, with the support of the City Engineering
Office; and,
iv. Periodic review of insurance coverage of all insurable assets to ensure that the
City’s properties are adequately protected against any damage to or loss due
to fire, earthquake, storm, or other fortuitous events/casualty in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018.

b. For the City Accounting Office to enhance practices in the reporting of accounts
by:

i. Making the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the appropriate
accumulated depreciation amount and come up with the correct value of the
PPE accounts;
ii. Revisiting all IIRUP together with the transmitted disposal documents, and
creating the necessary journal entries to drop from the books all PPE items
that have already disposed of and demolished; and,
iii. Disclosing the total Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements
to provide the end users with adequate information regarding the accounts
following the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008.

c. For the City Accountant and the General Services Officer to ensure that the
balances of the PPE accounts in their respective records are reconciled on a
regular basis and that discrepancies noted shall be immediately investigated and
resolved to maintain the proper valuation of accounts.

Furthermore, both Offices shall collaborate their efforts and practice regular
coordination to warrant a more accurate report of PPE balances.

We recommended that Management:

a. Regularly communicate and request documents such as amortization schedules
with banks and other lending institutions, to facilitate the amortization of the loans
availed by the LGU and the proper identification of the current and non-current
portion of the loans and other liability accounts, otherwise, use estimates based
on past principal and interest payments and other applicable sources of
information as basis in the computation, reclassification and recognition of loans
payable and accrued interest; and
b. Consider assigning a focal team to oversee loan documentation compliance from
start to approval, project implementation, and monitoring. Duplication efforts will
be minimized as the team focuses on connecting with lending institutions to
ensure current compliance and adequate record-keeping.

We recommended that Management:

a. Assess whether the PPAs originally funded are feasible to be implemented,
otherwise may consider reprogramming to more suitable PPAs in consonance
with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, and other pertinent guidelines to
encompass the four (4) thematic areas of DRRM such as disaster prevention and
mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response, and disaster rehabilitation
and recovery. Integrate them into the AIP for approval of the local Sanggunian to
serve as a roadmap in DRRMF utilization;
b. Monitor the unexpended balances to be recognized under the Trust Liabilities-
DRRM account including its utilization and balances. Ensure the accounting and
reporting guidelines are adhered to, based on COA Circular 2012-002 and RA
10121 known as the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of
2010; and
c. Through the CAO, draw journal entries on the unrecorded PPE items in the
General Fund books and be recognized with depreciation charges. Moving
forward, ensure that PPE accounts are properly recorded in the TF books and
transfer them in the GF once completed, issued to the end-user and ownership
has been transferred in adherence to COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that Management:

a. Through the Local School Board to assess the proposed plan to be included in the
annual and supplemental budget and align it with the priority expenditures
indicated in Republic Act No. 7160, as well as the DepED, DBM, and DILG Joint
Circulars No. 1, series of 2017 and No. 1, series of 2020;
b. Foster a collaborative partnership between the CGK and DepED to enhance the
budgeting and utilization procedures of the SEF, instituting control systems to
ensure the efficient delivery of services; and
c. Consider lining up other expenditures besides sports development for strategic
level decision-making and to effectively address the fund’s goal.

We recommended that Management:

a. Observe and regularly review the procedures for amending the Schedule of Fair
Market Value of real properties and general revisions of real property
assessments, and adhere to the appraisal/assessment calendar prescribed by the
Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations;
b. Establish a systematic and transparent process for conducting assessments,
including the use of updated valuation methodologies and technologies;
c. Strengthen information and dissemination campaign activities to raise citizens’
awareness of the need to revise the City Tax Ordinance and urge their support
and involvement; and
d. Once the proposed Ordinance has been enacted, publicize the approved SFMV in
a newspaper of general circulation in the locality or posting in the city hall and two
other conspicuous public places.

We recommended that Management:

a. Through the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP), consider prioritizing the adoption of
the CENRO’s proposed ordinance as it could initiate plans and actions for
implementing the septage management system in the locality;
b. Intensify the collaborative efforts with the MKWD in pursuit to provide a clean and
sufficient water supply in the area;
c. May team up with relevant agencies, such as Department of Environment and
Natural Resources’ Environmental Management Bureau (DENR-EMB), Department
of Health (DOH) for hospital waste management, and other LGUs for knowledge
sharing and benchmarking; and
d. Organize seminars or educational initiatives aimed at implementing water
conservation measures, advocating for the adoption of water-saving technologies,
and offering incentives to encourage both households and businesses to reduce
their water usage and proactively.

a. For the CAO to improve practices in the reporting of accounts by: • Recognizing depreciation expenses for all PPEs regardless of its year of acquisition; • Effecting the necessary adjusting entries for prior years’ misclassified PPE accounts and the derecognition of replaced asset
observations.managementAction: Recording of adjusting entries for all misclassified PPE accounts per JEV# 008751, 008757, 008796, 008797, 008813, 008366, 008409, 008426, 008490 & 008638; Recording of adjusting entries of misclassified PPE accounts and the derecognition of replaced asset following the provision of COA Circular 2015-008; Recording of adjusted depreciation entries for misclassified PPE accounts and the derecognition of replaced assets per JEV# 019367-369, 019371, 019376, 019381, 019383, 000731-735, 000720-721, 000710-714
observations.reason: The accumulated depreciation of misclassified PPE was adjusted in 2024. Formed part of Obs. No. 1 in CY 2023 AAR

observations.recommendation: a. Ensure that recorded AP and accruals are valid claims and properly supported with approved documentary requirements; b. Strictly observe the policies on payables and cut-off for the appropriate recording and setting up of accounts, and accountabilities as of year-end; c. Make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts for the AP recognized, but no actual goods were delivered nor transactions of a given date.
observations.managementAction: The CAO already initiated the thorough review of the claims to ensure that these are valid (LGU actually received the goods and services) and supported with documentary requirements. Obligations were assessed; items which can no longer be booked in 2023 were reverted.
observations.reason: There were some supplies delivered in 2023 but were reverted. The LGU established October 30, 2023 as cut-off period to cater purchase requests so that goods and services delivered before year-end will be booked as claims/payables. Necessary adjustments in the books were drawn.

observations.recommendation: a. Evaluate each line item under the Other Payables account whether there will be probable outflows, otherwise may consider re-programming of the funds to more suitable projects, programs, and activities (PPAs) needed by the constituents; b. Through the City Accounting Office: • Strictly monitor all booked obligations to ensure that these are valid claims. Follow-up the accountable persons for the sufficient and appropriate supporting documents; • Review and assess if those obligations can still be processed and can be paid to the payees; • If claims can no longer be established, take up in the books of accounts the payables which are beyond two years from date of recognition as surplus adjustment of the fund against which they were previously charged; and • Effect the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the accounts improperly booked in Other Payables to ensure that accounts are accurately presented in the FS; and c. Require all concerned departments which have collections like the Office of the City Building Official to prepare Work and Financial Plan (WFP) or Program of Works (POW) detailing the manner of utilization of the 15% share held under the TF for the whole year for approval of the Local Chief Executive, and submit a copy of the approved POW to the DPWH Secretary thru the National Building Code Development Office (NBCDO), as mandated in items 2.6 and 2.7 of the DILG and DPWH Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 001 dated July 4, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The CAO initiated the thorough review of the claims to ensure that these are valid (LGU actually received the goods and services) and supported with documentary requirements. Obligations were assessed, items which can no longer be booked in 2023 were reverted. Verbal communication has already been made to the concerned departments/users. A useful Work and Financial Plan / Program of Works was prepared where applicable.
observations.reason: Some payables were assessed and reverted. Further assessment shall be made for accounts remaining in the books. Attachments shall be reviewed when transactions occur. Inspection of inventory is temporarily done. Some of the required documents were furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: a. Set up detailed inventory control mechanisms that will capture the proper receipt, storage, issuance, monitoring/controlling and recording of inventories; b. Require all offices to submit promptly the documents pertaining to procurement of inventories for timely review and actual acceptance/inspection of the GSO; c. Through the GSO, assign a team that will focus on the observance of proper protocols as to the receipt, issuance and monitoring of items; d. Require periodic physical inventory and prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) for submission to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year; e. CAO and CGSO regularly reconcile balances per ledger cards and stock cards; f. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventories, with assistance of all offices, and to prepare the RPCI for submission to the City Accountant for necessary adjustments and then to the Auditor’s Office for review not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.
observations.managementAction: An online inventory system is already in place since April 2023. The GSO maintained warehousing controls and commenced construction of the GSO warehouse. Verbal communication has been made to departments/users to comply. A focal person has been assigned to properly monitor liquidation documents. The CAO is already using the asset method in recording purchases of supplies and materials.
observations.reason: Some areas in supply chain management shall be reinforced in CY 2024. The CAO and CGSO reconcile the balances per ledger and Report of Supplies and Materials Issued. Inspection of inventory is temporarily done. A section within the GSO with the proper manpower compliment is being established.

observations.recommendation: a. Set the GAD Agenda or identify priority gender issues, mandates or targets and ensure the provision of adequate resources; b. Include in the GPB appropriations for capability development programs to strengthen LGU personnel’s understanding of the GAD Programs and their roles in the mainstreaming into local policy-making, planning, programming, budgeting, implementation, monitoring, and evaluation; c. Revisit the Philippine Plan for Gender-Responsive Development 1995-2025, the Beijing Platform for Action, Ambisyon Nation 2040 and the Sustainable Development Goals which embodies programs under different sectors for replication of the LGU; d. May consider adding personnel assigned to plan, implement and monitor GAD PPAs on a full-time basis; e. Properly monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs to ensure efficiency in managing its resources and effective observance of its objectives; f. Adhere to item V of COA Circular 2014-001 for the timely submission of GAD-related reports to the COA audit team.
observations.managementAction: The City Government set the GAD mandates and targets and the GAD PPAs are being considered in the next planning period. The GAD Council assured to adhere to the said guidelines and will comply with the monitoring and evaluation team. The DepEd/other partners to be coordinated where applicable. No additional personnel were allocated as no office space was provided for GAD; periodic assessments of PPAs will be done.
observations.reason: PPAs under GAD were initially taken from the review of LGU Accomplishment Reports. Suggestions for inclusion will be considered in the next planning period. Some required documents were furnished to the Office of the Auditor. No additional personnel were allocated as no office space was provided for GAD. Firming up of monitoring and evaluation team to comply with other requirements.

observations.recommendation: b. Strictly implement the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, draw necessary accounting entries, measurements, presentation and disclosure requirements as provided in the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Formed part of Obs. No. 1 of CY2021 AAR

observations.recommendation: a. Through the City Agriculture and City Veterinary Office, assign a Biological Asset/s Caretaker or Officer-In –Charge that will closely monitor the assets and take note of its biological transformation by properly filling-out the Biological Assets Property Card (BAPC); and

Not Implemented
May no longer warrant the need to assign a BA Caretaker or OIC as an arrangement has been made to the supplier for dispersals.

observations.recommendation: a. Consider setting – up an Information Technology Office (ITO) or if not yet possible, may assign organic personnel who is equipped for initially reviewing as to how the system be designed, programmed and implemented in the LGU.

The internal auditors should be detached from regular functions or those of routine operating character as mentioned in Administrative Order No. 278 issued by then President Corazon C. Aquino in 1992; and

Not Implemented
Had benchmarking with General Santos City as to set-up of IAS, but budget was set-up for 2021.
Due to concentration of efforts in containing or mitigating the virus and its impact to communities, this plan of formally setting up the IAS is deferred.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
a. For the Local Chief Executive, to direct the concerned department/personnel
in ensuring full implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPE account
balances, draw necessary accounting entries, measurements, presentation
and disclosure requirements as provided in the COA Circular No. 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020 and ensure completeness of details in the Report on
the Physical Count of Property, Plant, & Equipment (RPCPPE);
b. For the Inventory Committee, to evaluate recorded items below the
capitalization criteria whether these are semi-expendable items misclassified
as PPE which shall have been expensed upon issuance or these are items with
individual values below threshold but which work together in the form of a
group of network asset;
c. For the City Accountant to improve practices in reporting of accounts by:
 Closely monitoring and reviewing the journal entries relating to Construction
in Progress (CIP) account and establish CIP account in Trust Fund and
provide adjusting journal entries of CIP if necessary; and
 Taking up the necessary journal entries to correct the balances of the net
book value of identified PPE accounts and Government Equity accounts
including reclassification of the semi-expendable items with costs not
exceeding capitalization threshold upon coordination with the Inventory
Committee.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Strictly monitor all booked obligations to ensure that these are valid claims.
Follow-up the accountable persons for sufficient and appropriate supporting
documents;
b. Review and assess if those obligations can still be processed and can be paid to
the payees; and
c. If claims can no longer be established, the Accountant take up in the books of
accounts the payables which are beyond two years from date of recognition as
surplus adjustment of the fund against which they were previously charged.

We recommended that Management:
a. May partner with relevant bodies such as Department of Information and
Communications Technology (DICT), Department of Interior and Local
Government (DILG), National ICT Confederation of the Philippines (NICP),
Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) and other LGUs for knowledge sharing and
benchmarking; and
b. Consider setting-up an Information Technology Office (ITO) or if not yet
possible, may assign organic personnel in the field of IT to initially review the
current flow of information. A collaboration across departments may also be
performed to grasp their requirements known as systems analysis before
proceeding to system design then development, integration and testing,
implementation and maintenance.

a. Review its duly instituted Inventory Committee. Tap more people to do the physical count and set schedule with the various departments/offices to ensure that complete physical inventory-taking be made. Assign one person in each department/office who will maintain the records of all property, plant and equipment issued to the concerned officers and employees to ensure timely and complete Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPPE);
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Although an memorandum was already issued, actual physical inventory-taking of properties shall mainly be responsible by the inventory committee.

observations.recommendation: c. Through the GSO, observe the necessary protocols in the disposal of unserviceable properties; and
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The Inventory and Appraisal Committee has started validating and updating the list of unserviceable properties. The BAC posted for bidding those items which may still be given scrap value.

observations.recommendation: d. Promptly prepare the necessary documents and submit the required reports to the Auditor’s Office not later than January 31 of each year for evaluation and validation.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Some of the reports require additional information.

observations.recommendation: a. Establish necessary internal control measures governing cash advances and ensures full dissemination and proper observance by all offices of the City Government;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: b. Require the accountable officers (AOs) to liquidate immediately the cash advances and adhere with the provisions of Section 83, Volume 1 casting of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs and COA Circular 97-002 to avoid accumulation of outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: c. Strictly observe the maximum cash accountabilities provided in the Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Some AOs have been provided increase in their accountabilities.
To date, there are 21 AOs covered with bond.

observations.recommendation: d. Consider assigning focal persons and be sufficiently bonded with the Bureau of the Treasury for major programs of the LGU who will be responsible for overseeing the implementation of the said program and proper liquidation of the CAs made for the purpose.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The management assigned accountable persons to be responsible for specific accounts for proper recording and custody of accountable forms.
Additional AO may still be considered to specify assignments, i.e. one person may be assigned for SEF purposes, another for GAD-related Activities, and the like.

observations.recommendation: e. Timely update the cash books and proper reconciliation with the Accounting Records be made; and
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Delays in updating of some cash books were observed.

observations.recommendation: f. Strict and periodic monitoring of liquidation report submission from the AOs to ensure appropriate accounting thereof and taking up expenses in the Financial Statements. Consider issuing demand letters and when no proper actions have been taken within the prescribed period , may cause the withholding of salaries in consonance to item 5.1 of COA Circular 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: a. Come up with detailed inventory control mechanisms that will capture the proper receipt, storage, issuance, monitoring/ controlling and recording of inventory items;
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Initial planning and/or meeting with heads of offices were made to discuss inventory control mechanisms they can apply.

observations.recommendation: b. Require all offices to submit promptly the documents pertaining to procurement of inventories for actual acceptance/inspection of the GSO;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: c. Through the City Accountant, set the actual inventories on hand at year end each commodity/stock and maintain perpetual ledger cards for each commodity/stock and General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain stock cards to account for the receipt and disposition of the same. CAO and CGSO regularly reconcile the balances per ledger card and stock cards;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: d. Issue supplies with approved Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) and be consolidated weekly by the GSO in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to be submitted to the city Accountant for preparation of the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the expenditures using appropriate expenditure account; and
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: e. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventories and prepare the report on Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) for submission to the City Accountant to effect necessary adjustments and the Auditor’s Office not later than January 31 of each year for perusal.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The management started assigning person per office who will take charge of counting and monitoring of inventories.
Monitoring and count of inventories will be instensified in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: b. Include the physical count of biological assets at year-end and reconcile with the BAPC. Discrepancies shall be immediately verified for proper disposition. Necessary documents, including status reports be forwarded to the City Accounting Office for proper recording in the books of accounts and appropriate disclosures be made.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat shall strictly comply with the reporting and posting requirements enumerated under GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and DBM LC No. 125 to promote transparency and accountability and aid the oversight agencies obtain adequate information for timely and relevant decision-making activity.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: a. Re-assess the unexpended balances in the STF and update local DRRM Plan and Fund Investment Plan to include the new PPAs in maximizing achievement of program objectives; and;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: b. Strictly comply with the accounting and reporting requirements embodied in R.A. 10121, COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 to ensure that the financial information is appropriately taken up in the books with adequate disclosures. The same reports shall be posted in the LGU website and full disclosure portal of the DILG.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Some forms and reports may still be adopted, while disclosures can be added.

observations.recommendation: c. Recommend strategies and programs for appropriation for climate change adaptation and mitigation. Advocacy for the effective implementation of the building code use of green technology, conduct of inventory, vulnerability and risk assessments for critical facilities and infrastructure are to be included for building a safe, disaster-resilient and adaptive community.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: d. Want of Inventory Items disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements

b. Through the City Human Resource Management Office, evaluate the existing manpower complement to check possible transfer of personnel to form the IAS/IAU, revise staffing patterns or create new internal auditor positions in accordance with CSC Memorandum Circular No. 19 s. 1992 and other relevant issuances.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Initial review and/or evaluation was already made, persons of interest were also considered. An initial assignment was made.
Some employees were assigned to be performing functions relevant to the Internal Audit Service / Unit.

observations.recommendation:
a. Formally set up the Internal Audit Service / Unit with a city government department head, SG-25. The internal auditors should be detached from regular functions or those of routine operating character as mentioned in Administrative Order No. 278 issued by then President Corazon C. Aquino in 1992; and
b. Through the City Human Resource Management Office, evaluate the existing manpower complement to check possible transfer of personnel to form the IAS/IAU, revise staffing patterns or create new internal auditor positions in accordance with CSC Memorandum Circular No. 19 s. 1992 and other relevant issuances.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2019 Initial review and/or evaluation was already made, persons of interest were also considered.
observations.reason: Due to concentration of efforts in containing or mitigating the virus and its impact to communities, this plan of formally setting up the IAS is deferred.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following to the
management, some of which were reiteration of the prior years’ audit
recommendations:

a. Review its duly constituted Inventory Committee. Tap more people to do the
physical count and set schedule with the various departments/offices to ensure
that complete physical inventory-taking be made. Assign one person in each
department/office who will maintain the records of all property, plant, and
equipment issued to the concerned officers and employees to ensure timely and
complete Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant, & Equipment
(RCPPE);
b. Strictly implement the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, draw
necessary accounting entries, measurements, presentation and disclosure
requirements as provided in the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,
2020;
c. Through the GSO, observe the necessary protocols in the disposal of
unserviceable properties; and
d. Promptly prepare the necessary documents and submit the required reports to
the Auditor’s Office not later than January 31 of each year for evaluation and
validation.

We recommended that Management:

a. Establish necessary internal control measures governing cash advances and
ensure full dissemination and proper observance by all offices of the City
Government;
b. Require the accountable officers (AOs) to liquidate immediately the cash
advances and adhere with the provisions of Section 83, Volume I of the
Government Accounting Manual for LGUs and COA Circular 97-002 to
avoid accumulation of outstanding cash advances;
c. Strictly observe the maximum cash accountabilities provided in Treasury
Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009;
d. Consider assigning focal persons, who are sufficiently bonded with the
Bureau of Treasury, for major programs of the LGU who will be responsible
for overseeing the implementation of the said program and proper
liquidation of the CAs made for the purpose;
e. Timely update the cash books and proper reconciliation with the Accounting
records be made; and
f. Strict and periodic monitoring of liquidation report submission from the
AOs to ensure appropriate accounting thereof and taking up of expenses in
the Financial Statements. Consider issuing demand letters and when no
proper actions have been taken within the prescribed period, may cause the
withholding of salaries in consonance to item 5.1 of COA Circular 97-002.

We recommended that Management:

a. Come up with detailed inventory control mechanisms that will capture the
proper receipt, storage, issuance, monitoring/controlling and recording of
inventory items;
b. Require all offices to submit promptly the documents pertaining to
procurement of inventories for timely review and actual acceptance/inspection
of the GSO;
c. Through the City Accountant, maintain perpetual ledger cards for each
commodity/stock and the General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain stock
cards to account for the receipt and disposition of the same. CAO and CGSO
regularly reconcile the balances per ledger card and stock cards;
d. Issue supplies with approved Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) and be
consolidated weekly by the GSO in the Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) to be submitted to the City Accountant for preparation of the
Journal Voucher (JV) to record the expenditures using appropriate
expenditure account; and
e. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventories and
prepare the Report on Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) for submission to
the City Accountant to effect necessary adjustments and the Auditor’s Office
not later than January 31 of each year for perusal.

We recommended that Management:

a. Through the City Agriculture and City Veterinary Office, assign a Biological
Asset/s Caretaker or Officer-In-Charge that will closely monitor the assets and
take note of its biological transformation by properly filling-out the Biological
Assets Property Card (BAPC); and
b. Include the physical count of biological assets at year-end and reconcile with
the BAPC. Discrepancies shall be immediately verified for proper disposition.
Necessary documents, including status reports be forwarded to the City
Accounting Office for proper recording in the books of accounts and
appropriate disclosures be made.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat
shall strictly comply with the reporting and posting requirements enumerated
under GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and DBM LBC No. 125 to promote
transparency and accountability and aid the oversight agencies obtain adequate
information for a timely and relevant decision-making activity.

We recommended that Management:

a. Re-assess the unexpended balances in the STF and update local DRRM Plan
and Fund Investment Plan to include the new PPAs in maximizing achievement
of program objectives; and
b. Strictly comply with the accounting and reporting requirements embodied in
R.A. 10121, COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and COA
Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 to ensure that the financial information
is appropriately taken up in the books with adequate disclosures. The same
reports shall be posted in the LGU website and full disclosure portal of the
DILG.

We recommended that Management:

a. Consider setting-up an Information Technology Office (ITO) or if not yet
possible, may assign organic personnel who is equipped for initially reviewing as
to how the system be designed, programmed and implemented in the LGU by
phase;
b. May also partner with other relevant bodies for the purpose, to encourage
knowledge sharing and benchmarking; and
c. Develop an initial policy for the use and maintenance of the system embedding
internal control mechanisms to ensure security in access and limitation of
actions allowed for employees, even before it becomes operational.

a. Establish an Inventory Committee for proper management and control of all LGU properties. Reconcile the results of the count of the properties with the accounting records to ensure correctness and accuracy of the recorded/reported PPE in the FS as well as establish accountability. And observe prompt submission of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RPCPPE);
b. Allocate budget for premium insurance and direct the General Services Office to prepare and accomplish the Property Insurance Form (PIF) for uninsured properties. Insure against any insurable risk the properties, assets, and interests with the GIF, as administered by the GSIS the soonest possible time to protect agency assets against losses due to fire, earthquake and other calamities.
observations.managementAction: Issued Executive Order No. 083, s.2020 creating Inventory Committee, providing for its composition, duties and functions

Instructed the Office of the City Engineer to regularly provide the Offices of the City GSO the City Accountant the needed data. Some reports have been initially made, proper tie up or reconcilitation still be sought.
observations.reason: Record maintenance and various intervening activities, including limitations in duty caused by the pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
a. Through the City Accountant maintain perpetual inventory records for each commodity/stock and the General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain stock cards to account for the receipt and disposition of the same. The balance per stock card/property ledger cards should always reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting unit; and
b. Issue supplies with approved Requisition and Issue Slip and be consolidated weekly by the GSO in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to be submitted to the City Accountant for preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the expenditures using appropriate expenditure account.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2019 Recognition of inventories in the books of accounts and reconciliation with GSO were made. Formed part of Obs. No. 2 in CY 2020
observations.reason: Non-maintenance of complete Inventory Ledger Card (ILC) and Stock Cards and reconciliation with GSO were considered to be major factors of non-preparation/submission of inventory reports delayed

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the City Accountant to review the Journal Entry Voucher in taking up transfer of funds to ensure that all reciprocal transactions are accurately recorded in the books of accounts. Moreover, prepare the necessary adjustment to reflect the correct balances for the reciprocal accounts.
observations.managementAction: Submitted copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers to adjust and reflect correct balances of accounts.

AAR CY 2019 Projects were already bidded out in 2020 and for implementation. Some PPAs were initially evaluated. Others are suspended as much of the funds remain untapped. Considers clear-cut guidelines by having a MOA with the source agencies.
observations.reason: Changes were made on some PPAs for which funds remain untapped, while others were augmented by the LGU to be pushed through this 2021 onwards.

observations.recommendation:
a. Thoroughly review the required attachments embodied in R.A. 9184 and COA Circular 2009-001. A transmittal letter detailing for the list of documents shall be provided and the same be index-tabbed for easy-reference.
b. Submit the documents in its original form. Two sets are required for those contracts above threshold - ₱2M for goods and services; and ₱5M for infrastructure projects as these are subject to review and inspection of COA Regional Office XII Technical Services Office (TSO).
c. Observe the timeline for submission of contracts, POs and supporting documents for review, evaluation and inspection of projects, programs and activities (PPAs) implemented by the Agency. And within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of goods, notify the Auditor of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat reviews the documents and ensures completion before submission to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Documents provided were in original form, but provision of two sets for contracts above threshold are yet to be fully embraced.

observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2019 Reports were submitted to the Office of the Auditor and copies were also furnished to the donors for transparency. The LGU has integrated these requisites in their local DRRM plan and development plan. CPMC is conducting monitoring of projects twice a month.
observations.reason: As funds have been utilized and are continually being used to sustain the pressing needs of the community, more so, to beat and/or prevent the spread of COVID-19, stretching out of implementation of green technology projects and other capital expenditures are expected.

observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2019 PPAs were fast-tracked. Hired additional eight (8) engineers to strictly supervise project implementation. CPMC is conducting monitoring of projects twice a month.
observations.reason: The extent of damages, together with the limitations and challenges posed by the present pandemic demands much from the LGU officials and employees being the front-liners.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a. Establish an Inventory Committee for proper management and control of all
LGU properties. Reconcile the results of the count of the properties with the
accounting records to ensure correctness and accuracy of the recorded/reported
PPE in the FS as well as establish accountability. And observe prompt
submission of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant, and
Equipment (RPCPPE); and
b. Allocate budget for premium insurance and direct the General Services Office to
prepare and accomplish the Property Insurance Form (PIF) for uninsured
properties. Insure against any insurable risk the properties, assets, and interests
with the GIF, as administered by the GSIS the soonest possible time to protect
agency assets against losses due to fire, earthquake and other calamities.

We recommended that Management:

a. Through the Office of the City Accountant, adjust the affected accounts that are
determined to be understated or overstated to present the correct balances and
the necessary disclosures in the financial statements;
b. Require the City Engineer to provide the City Accountant and GSO the
necessary information for cost segregation of road projects; and
c. Prepare the necessary documents and submit the required reports to the
Auditor’s Office not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that Management:

a. Through the City Accountant maintain perpetual inventory records for each
commodity/stock and the General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain stock
cards to account for the receipt and disposition of the same. The balance per
stock card/property cards should always reconcile with the ledger cards of the
accounting unit; and
b. Issue supplies with approved Requisition and Issue Slip and be consolidated
weekly by the GSO in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to
be submitted to the City Accountant for preparation of the Journal Entry
Voucher (JEV) to record the expenditures using appropriate expenditure
account.

We recommended for the City Accountant to review the Journal Entry
Voucher in taking up transfer of funds to ensure that all transactions are accurately
recorded in the books of accounts. Moreover, prepare the necessary adjustment to
reflect the correct balances for the reciprocal accounts.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the respective Project-In-Charge to (i) prepare Final or Terminal
Reports for completed projects for submission to the City Accountant; and (ii)
cause the immediate completion of the remaining projects. Make a
representation with the source NGAs and propose that funds remaining for
completed projects may be used to finance subsequent related project/s of
improvement of the original projects already completed or if the said fund may
be transferred to the General Fund as available for the LGU’s disposition,
otherwise the same be remitted to the SA to be cleared from the books of
accounts;
b. Make a detailed evaluation on the feasibility of the unimplemented PPAs for
which funds from the National Government Agencies (NGAs) were earmarked.
If implementation be proved to be doubtful or no longer necessary, may
consider preparing a different request or proposal as a counter-offer before
returning the funds to the source agencies (SA) otherwise, said funds shall be
returned to the SAs; and
c. For subsequent fund transfers, timely monitor the implementation of PPAs in
consonance with the signed Memoranda of Agreement (MOA) to realize
program objectives beneficial to the community and avoid lodging amounts in
the LGU financial statements.

We recommended that Management:

a. Thoroughly review the required attachments embodied in R.A. 9184 and COA
Circular 2009-001. A transmittal letter detailing the list of documents shall be
provided and the same be index-tabbed for easy-reference.
b. Submit the documents in its original form. Two sets are required for those
contracts above threshold - ₱2M for goods and services; and ₱5M for
infrastructure projects as these are subject to review and inspection of COA
Regional Office XII Technical Services Office (TSO).
c. Observe the timeline for submission of contracts, POs and supporting
documents for review, evaluation and inspection of projects, programs and
activities (PPAs) implemented by the Agency. And within twenty-four (24) hours
from acceptance of goods, notify the Auditor of the time and date of the
scheduled deliveries.

We recommended that Management:

a. Formally set up the Internal Audit Service / Unit with a city government
department head, SG-25. The internal auditors should be detached from
regular functions or those of routine operating character as mentioned in
Administrative Order No. 278 issued by then President Corazon C. Aquino in
1992; and
b. Through the City Human Resource Management Office, evaluate the existing
manpower complement to check possible transfer of personnel to form the
IAS/IAU, revise staffing patterns or create new internal auditor positions in
accordance with CSC Memorandum Circular No. 19 s. 1992 and other relevant
issuances.

We recommended that Management:

a. Document accumulated experiences of communities from previous disasters
together with inputs from concerned agencies like MGB and PHIVOLCS in
updating the LGU’s Comprehensive Public Safety Manual, local DRRM Plan
and Fund Investment Plan to design PPAs for the unexpended balances, and
contingency plan to achieve a sustainable disaster management system;
b. Strictly comply with the accounting and reporting requirements embodied in
R.A. 10121, COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and COA
Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 to ensure that the financial information
is appropriately taken up in the books with adequate disclosures. The same
reports shall be posted in the LGU website and full disclosure portal of the
DILG; and
c. Recommend strategies and programs for appropriation for climate change
adaptation and mitigation. Advocacy for the implementation of the building
code and use of green technology, conduct of inventory, vulnerability and risk
assessments for critical facilities and infrastructure are to be included for
building a safe, disaster-resilient and adaptive community.

We recommended that Management:

a. Coordinate with the Local Development Council and Local Finance Committee
to plan out feasible PPAs for the funds remaining untapped;
b. Evaluate the sufficiency and capacity of employed engineers and support staff to
strictly supervise the implementation of PPAs. Actual inspection and report
formulation impact timely evaluation and impose commitment from contracting
parties. Check on applying “geo-tagging” for real-time monitoring and easy
access or update of project accomplishments; and
c. Require the CPMC to aptly conduct periodic monitoring and evaluation of the
City’s PPA implementation with appropriate reports submitted to the City
Mayor for perusal and appropriate action.
We recommended that Management should prudently and judiciously book up the RPT/SET Receivables to ensure inclusion of deliquencies.
observations.managementAction: City Accountant had already drawn JEV No. 200-2019-03-001596 on March 31, 2019 and JEV No. 100-2019-04-04969 to record the understatement of RPT/SET delinquencies and collectibles.

observations.recommendation: We recommeded that Management should look into the propriety of the long outstanding payable accounts, confirm the existence of the various payables and the validity of their respective claims and documentation and, if found to be unsupported with valid claims and documentation, revert the outstanding balance to the City Government’s General Fund account.
observations.managementAction: Entries to record salaries and benefits at year-end 2019 will also be taken so as to record corresponding payees and the validity of their respective claims and documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for Management, in coordination with the Local Development Council and Local Finance Committee, to promote/encourage full utilization of the 20% DF. Ensure timely preparation of Program of Works and other required documentations to facilitate timely and programmed implementation of development projects. Moreover, specify the implementation schedule of the development projects in the AIP to provide a concrete basis for work plans, and to serve as a guide in procurement planning.
observations.managementAction: Re-strategize. Also to specify scheduled dates to when to implement the project(s). Formed part of Observation No. 9 in CY 2019.
observations.reason: Due to various intervening circumstances during 2019, to include the calamity that strike the LGU. Postponement in program implementation, series of re-programming to cater priority PPAs, heavy workload from the respective personnel involved as attention was concentrated in the disaster relief and rehabilitation brought limitation in the full utilization of the fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management should prudently and judiciously book
up the RPT/SET Receivables to ensure inclusion of deliquencies.

We recommeded that Management should look into the propriety of the long
outstanding payable accounts, confirm the existence of the various payees and the
validity of their respective claims and, if found to be unsupported with valid claims and
documentation, revert the outstanding balance to the City Government’s General Fund
account.

We recommended for Management, in coordination with the Local Development
Council and Local Finance Committee, to promote/encourage full utilization of the 20%
DF. Ensure timely preparation of Program of Works and other required documentations
to facilitate timely implementation of development projects. Moreover, specify the
implementation schedule of the development projects in the AIP to provide a concrete
basis for work plans, and to serve as a guide in procurement planning.

We recommend that the
management refrain
from charging expenditures not within
the bounds of laws and
regulations on SEF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
management prepare
adjusting entries to
correct the affected
balances in the books of
accounts. Henceforth,
record the costs of road
maintenance projects to
its proper accounts.
