observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management prepare adjusting entries to correct the
affected balances in the books of accounts. Henceforth, record the costs of road
maintenance projects to its proper accounts.

We recommended that the management adjust the affected accounts that are
determined to be overstated in accordance with PPSAS 17 to present the correct balances
in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Accounting and Treasury Departments exhaust all
possible efforts to locate the documents supporting the discrepancies noted in the
reconciliation of bank accounts. Moreover, the discrepancies noted in the Bank
Reconciliation Statements should be adjusted immediately to correct the balances of the
Cash in Bank – Local Currency accounts.

We recommended that the management through the City Treasurer’s Office exert
extra effort in verifying the accuracy of the declared gross sales/receipts of all businesses
within the City of Kidapawan by examining their books and other pertinent records in
order to ascertain, assess and collect the correct amount of tax due.

We recommended that the management refrain from charging expenditures not
within the bounds of laws and regulations on SEF.

We recommended the following:
a. The Inventory Committee shall coordinate closely with the City Accountant in
reconciling the results of the inventory with those recorded in the Registries of
Public Infrastructures;
b. The CGSO and City Engineer shall perform its duties and responsibilities in
accordance with Items VI and VII of COA Circular No. 2015-008; and
c. The City Accountant shall recognize in the books of accounts the reconciled
amount of road networks that are recorded in the registries.

Instruct the City Property Custodian to conduct an actual inventory of Animals/Breeding Stocks, prepare and submit immediately the necessary report in order for the accounting unit to record any possible adjustments affecting the condition of the animal concerned to reflect a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Conducted actual Inventor with the City Veterinary Office Prepared & submitted the necessary inventory reports on the Work Animals/Breeding Stocks to the Accounting office for adjustment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Accounting and Treasury Departments exhaust all possible efforts to locate the documents supporting the discrepancies noted in the reconciliation of bank accounts.
We further recommend that the discrepancies noted in the Bank Reconciliation Statements should be adjusted immediately if necessary to corrects the balances of the Cash in Banks Accounts.
observations.managementAction: Verified & not find any documents to support the unreconciled items. To continue effort in locatinf supporting documents, if any
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the report

observations.recommendation:
The Accountant shall: pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 9, 2016.
1. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of existence of the receivables, unliquidated cash advances and funds transfers and determine the concern debtors, accountable officers (Regular and Special Disbursing Officers, Collecting Officers, Cashiers) and the source and implementing government entities concerned; Prepare Aging of other Receivables as of June 30, 2017
2. Reconcile the unliquidated fund transfer between the source and implementing government entities and prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted;
3. Prepare aging of dormant receivables, unliquidated cash advances and fund transfer on a quarterly basis to support the request for write-off, and indicated in the remarks column the existence of applicable conditions, as follows:
a. Absence of records or documents to validated/support the claim and/ or unreconciled reciprocal accounts
b. Death of the accountable officer/ employee/ debtor.
c. Unknown whereabouts of the accountable officer/ employee/ debtor and that he/she could not be located despite diligent efforts to find him/her.
d. Incapacity to pay or insolvency.
e. Exhaustion of possible remedies by the management to collect the receivables and to demand the liquidation of cash advances and fund transfers.
f. No pending case in court involving the subject dormant account.
observations.managementAction: Necessary adjustments prepared w/in May 8, 2017. Prepared & submitted to COA on June 15, 2017. Submit to COA on June 5, 2017. To verify addresses w/ offices/persons who know the concern debtors. Aging of other Receivables as of June 30, 2017.
observations.reason: The Accounting Dept. still on the process verifying/locating addresses of concerned debtors.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Acting City Accountant should review the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to ensure that all transactions are recorded properly and accurately in the books of accounts, moreover, analyze the accounts and prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances in the books of accounts, Due from and Due to other Funds.
observations.managementAction: Necessary adjustments prepared w/in May 8-12, 2017

observations.recommendation:
1. We recommend that AOM management, in coordination with the Local Development Council to promote/encourage optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund. Monitoring of these project and fast tract the implementation with the aid of City Planning and Development Office is enjoined to fully achieved the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcome and ensure that the public benefits timely from these projects.
2. It is recommended further that management refrain from charging expenditures which are not in accordance with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 dated April 13, 2011.
3. We recommend that management, in coordination with the Local Development Council to promote/encourage optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund.
4. It is recommended further that management refrain from charging expenditures which are not in accordance with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: Implement PPAs funded under the 20% EDF
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that AOM management fast tract the implementation of the programs/projects of the city so that intended beneficiaries can enjoy the projects intended to them
observations.managementAction: Implement the said projects in CY 2017
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that AOM management intensify efforts in the establishment and Manually generate a gender-disaggregated database containing all sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics in compliance with Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Establish a Community Based Monitoring System. Manually generate a gender-disaggregated database containing all sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics in compliance with Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: Instruct the City Property Custodian to conduct an actual inventory of Work Animals/Breeding Stocks, prepare and submit immediately the necessary report in order for the accounting unit to record any possible adjustments affecting the condition of the animal concerned to reflect a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Accounting and Treasury Departments exhaust all possible efforts to locate the documents supporting the discrepancies noted in the reconciliation of bank accounts. We further recommend that, the discrepancies noted in the of Bank Reconciliation Statements should be adjusted immediately if necessary to correct the balances of the Cash in Bank Accounts.

It is recommended that the Acting City Accountant should review the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to ensure that all transactions are properly and accurately recorded in the books of accounts, moreover, analyze the accounts
and prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances in the books of accounts, Due from and Due to other Funds.

The Accountant shall: pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 9, 2016.
Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of existence of the receivables, unliquidated cash advances and funds transfers and determine the concern debtors, accountable officers (Regular and Special Disbursing Officers, Collecting Officers, Cashiers) and the source and implementing government entities concerned;
Reconcile the unliquidated fund transfer between the source and implementing government entities and prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted;
Prepare aging of dormant receivables, unliquidated cash advances and fund transfer on a quarterly basis to support the request for write-off, and indicate in the remarks column the existence of applicable conditions, as follows:
Absence of records or documents to validate/support the claim and/or unreconciled reciprocal accounts.
Death of the accountable officer/employee/debtor.
Unknown whereabouts of the accountable officer/employee/debtor and that he/she could not be located despite diligent efforts to find him/her.
Incapacity to pay or insolvency.
Exhaustion of possible remedies by the management to collect the receivables and to demand the liquidation of cash advances and fund transfers.
No pending case in court involving the subject dormant account.

We recommend  that  management, in   coordination  with  the  Local   Development
Council to promote/encourage optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund.  Monitoring of these project and fast tract the implementation with the aid of City Planning and Development Office is enjoined to fully achieved the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcome and ensure that the public benefits timely from these projects.
It is recommended further that management refrain from charging expenditures which are  not in accordance with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 dated April 13, 2011.
We recommend that management fast tract the implementation of the programs/projects of the city so that intended beneficiaries can enjoy the projects intended to them.

We recommend that management  intensify efforts in the establishment and maintenance of GAD database containing all sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics in compliance with Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

observations.managementAction:
Accounting Office have coordinated with the city Treasurer and agreed to rectify the deficiencies as cited for CY 2015 RPT and SET.  
Accounting Dept. have started the maintenance of subsidiary ledgers on the 5th of Oct. 2016.
Accounting Dept. to set up Real Property Tax and SET tax receivables from each taxpayers.
The Treasurer’s office  have furnish the accounting department of the duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the actual due and collectible for the year in compliance to recommendatin.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting and Treasury Departments exhaust all possible efforts to locate the documents supporting the discrepancies noted in the reconciliation of bank accounts. We further recommended that, if warranted, Management initiates the conduct of investigation to thresh out cause/s of discrepancy/ies that may be useful in making appropriate adjustment on the Cash in Bank records. Likewise, strengthen internal control system to ensure fulfillment, among others, of the officers/employees’ accountability obligations.
observations.managementAction: City Accounting Office have coordinated with the Office of the City Treasurer concerning stale checks which were included in the list of outstanding checks.  Conducted meeting with the City Government’s depository bank to discuss undetermined reconciling items.  The  Office of the City Accountant have secured bank certification on the audited balance of the different accounts of the City Government.
observations.reason: The bank and the Book reconciling items can  not be adjusted due to the absence/non availability of records/supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management creates a Committee on Appraisal and Disposal in accordance with Executive Order No. 888 and other pertinent law, rules and regulations and to undertake the disposal in accordance thereto.
observations.managementAction: The Office of City General Service have already submitted Waste Materials Report of unserviceable//obsolete  properties of the  office of the City Accountant. Public auction of the unserviceable properties was conductedon May 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management requires the concerned accountable officers to immediately liquidate their cash advances strictly in accordance with the law, rules and regulations.  Refrain from granting additional cash advances to Officers and Employees with outstanding cash advances pursuant to  Section 4.1.2 of COA  Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Liquidated on various dates

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management directs the City Treasurer to submit the remaining twenty eight (28) paid Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents amounting to P1,820,911.24. Likewise, ensures the prompt submission of the Report of Checks Issued with the paid Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents, Journal Entry Vouchers and pertinent papers within the time frame prescribed in existing regulations by the responsible officer/personnel with instruction to record transactions in the books of accounts in accordance with the procedures set forth in the New Government Accounting System.
observations.managementAction: Management had partially submitted the disbursement vouchers.  The remaining disbursement vouchers were not  forwarded yet to the City Treasurer’s Office to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management initiates the return of the unutilized balances amounting to P248,759.24 to the respective Source Agency in accordance with Section 6.7 of COA Circular 94-013 dated December 31, 1994 and provisions of Memorandum of Agreement between the Implementing Agency  and the Source Agency.
observations.managementAction: Management when asked  on this observation, assured to return the unutilized balance to the respective source agency. As noted, they are now on the process of preparing the necessary documents for the refund of said funds.
observations.reason: As of Dec. 31, 2016 a balance of P8,446.00 still unremitted.
